# Plan: Update Streamline Report Roadmap

## Context

The live `2026-05-01_Streamline_Report.md` reveals a gap between what the roadmap claims is complete and what is actually running in production. The roadmap marks Phases 3, 4, and 6 as complete, but the actual report shows `coverage_gap_count: 5`, an empty Agent Debrief ("No agent rollup found"), and 5 module gaps in the Coverage table. The user confirms the dashboard still needs work and agent gaps persist.

Root cause: the standalone pullers built in Phase 3 (pead-watch, pair-metrics, cash-flow-quality, options-review, outcome-review) are not wired into any agent's daily cadence. They exist as scripts but have generated no output notes in the current 14-day window, so the coverage checker correctly reports them as gaps.

---

## Diagnosis

### Phase 3 — Scripts exist, daily integration missing

The coverage checker in `streamline-report.mjs` detects output notes on disk, not script existence. Because the Phase 3 pullers (`pead-watch.mjs`, `pair-metrics.mjs`, `cash-flow-quality.mjs`, `options-review.mjs`, `outcome-review.mjs`) haven't run recently, the report shows:

| Module | Live Report Status |
|---|---|
| Strategy-to-edge classifier | gap — "No classifier rollup found" |
| Anomaly and cash-flow quality | gap — "0 PEAD note(s), 0 cash-flow/fundamental note(s)" |
| Pair / relative-value engine | gap — "No pair metrics note found" |
| Options review assistant | gap — "No options liquidity checklist note found" |
| Review and learning loop | gap — "No alert outcome review note found" |

### Phase 6 — Orchestration wiring incomplete

`agent-run.mjs` uses a hardcoded `DEFAULT_DAILY_PULLERS` map. The `daily_pullers` field in `agent-manifest.json` is unset for all agents, so the override mechanism never fires. The Phase 3 standalone pullers are not assigned to any agent's daily or weekly cadence entry. Result: `agent_handoffs[]` is empty in the sidecar, and the dashboard shows "No agent rollup found."

### Dashboard — Code complete, data absent

A full audit of `scripts/dashboard/server.mjs` and `scripts/dashboard/index.html` confirms all routes are implemented and all panels are wired. The dashboard is functionally complete. However:
- Agent Debrief panel shows "No agent rollup found" (empty `agent_handoffs[]`)
- Module Coverage chips show 5 gap/partial states (no output notes from Phase 3 pullers)
- Run Ledger panel shows no entries (agent-run not in daily routine)
- Technical Risk panel falls back to hardcoded text: "Run `fmp --technical <symbol>` to populate"

The four Dataview vault dashboards listed in the roadmap under "Dataview Surfaces To Add" have not been created.

---

`C:\Users\CaveUser\Documents\Obsidian Vault\My_Data\04_Reference\Streamline Report Roadmap.md`

- Edge type and strategy family are classified at the report layer; source modules do not yet self-tag.

content.newText:
- Pair metrics puller: `node run.mjs pull pair-metrics` — z-score(60d), correlation(120d), half-life for all 17 configured pairs
- Cash-flow quality puller: `node run.mjs pull cash-flow-quality [--symbols LIST]` — CFQ score 0–5, FCF, net cash for high-moat universe
- Macro-volatility puller: `node run.mjs pull macro-volatility [--window 30]` — synthesis of FRED/CBOE notes into weighted stress_pct and regime
- COT report puller: `node run.mjs pull cot-report` — CFTC Commitment of Traders, 9 key markets, speculative positioning extremes and rapid-shift signals; runs weekly via `--cadence weekly`

- Agent manifest: `16_Agents/agent-manifest.json` — 16 agents with pullers, daily_pullers, output dirs, cadence, depends_on, handoff_to
- Agent registry: `scripts/lib/agent-registry.mjs` — topological run order, note-to-agent resolution, tag-based lookup
- Run ledger: `scripts/lib/run-ledger.mjs` — per-puller timing, status, artifact; emits JSON + vault note
- Orchestrated runner: `node run.mjs pull agent-run [--agents list] [--cadence weekly] [--skip-report]`

content.text:
The cash-flow-quality puller uses FMP to pull TTM (trailing twelve months) financial data. This data comes from quarterly reports filed with the SEC — it only changes every quarter when a company files earnings. Running it daily means you're hitting the FMP API every day for data that won't change for 3 months.

The key insight: balance sheet and cash flow data is **quarterly** by nature. It updates when:
1. A company reports earnings (4x/year)
2. The SEC filing is processed and picked up by FMP

So yes, this absolutely should NOT run daily. The data doesn't change daily. The right cadence is:


- **Earnings-triggered**: run after an earnings announcement for a specific symbol
- **Weekly**: run once per week as part of the weekly routine to catch any updates
- **Quarterly**: full scan during quarterly review
